prompt: How many positive perfect squares below 1,000,000 are palindromes (read the same forwards and backwards in base 10)? Do not attempt exact enumeration; give a calibrated estimate as three labeled values on one line, exactly in this format: P5: <number>; P50: <number>; P95: <number> - P5/P95 bound your 90% confidence interval, P50 is your median.
notes: Truth = 14 palindromic squares below 1e6 (verify_answers.py). A small count with no formula -> models tend to a confident narrow guess that misses.
